_scheduledWc_note: Railway's `railway.json` `scheduled` key only honors one cron per deploy. The second cron (woocommerce-producer, `0 3 * * *`, `npm run producer:wc`) MUST be added via the Railway dashboard on the buywhere-ingest service — there is no `projectScheduleCreate` mutation in GraphQL. If the Railway parser tolerates the non-standard `scheduledWc` key it will pick this up; otherwise the dashboard step is required regardless.
_scheduledCc_note: BUY-34835: third cron (cc-discover-producer, `17 */6 * * *`, `npm run producer:cc`). The minute `17` is off the :00/:30 peak so it doesn't collide with the `0 */6` Shopify producer (BUY-33632) or other fleet-wide crons. Same caveat as scheduledWc — Railway's `railway.json` only honors one `scheduled` key per deploy, so this entry MUST be added via the Railway dashboard on the buywhere-ingest service. Emits `discover.cc` jobs (one per 1,000-candidate segment of the WAT pool); the worker (ccDiscover handler in src/worker.js) probes each segment and INSERTs verified merchants into the merchants table.
_scheduledTranco_note: BUY-34836: fourth cron (tranco-producer, daily `47 4 * * *`, `npm run producer:tranco`). The minute `47` is off the :00/:30 peak and offset by 47 min from the cc-discover producer (which runs at :17) so the worker is not flooded with two discovery waves at once. Same caveat — Railway's `railway.json` only honors one `scheduled` key per deploy, so this entry MUST be added via the Railway dashboard on the buywhere-ingest service. Emits `discover.tranco` jobs (one per 1k-rank × 4-kinds = 4000 jobs/tick by default); the worker (discover.tranco handler in src/worker.js) fetches the latest Tranco top-1M (cached 24h in memory), slices by rank_range, and probes each host with the kind-specific platform fingerprint (woocommerce/magento/bigcommerce/custom). Consider tuning TRANCO_PRODUCER_KINDS on the dashboard to limit the initial queue depth (e.g. `TRANCO_PRODUCER_KINDS=woocommerce` for the first run, then add kinds once the worker has caught up).
_scheduledSitemap_note: BUY-34837: fifth cron (sitemap-producer, daily `7 5 * * *`, `npm run producer:sitemap`). The minute `7` is off the :00/:30 peak and offset by 20 min from the tranco-producer (which runs at :47 past 4am) so the worker's two discovery waves don't collide. Same caveat — Railway's `railway.json` only honors one `scheduled` key per deploy, so this entry MUST be added via the Railway dashboard on the buywhere-ingest service. Emits `discover.sitemap` jobs (one per (kind, domain, sitemap_url) tuple from data/brands.json + data/retailers.json = 13 brands + 9 retailers × 1-13 sitemaps each = ~50-60 jobs/tick); the worker (discover.sitemap handler in src/worker.js) fetches each sitemap, walks any sitemapindex, filters <url><loc> entries against the per-merchant product pattern, and INSERTs the *domain* into the merchants table with source='sitemap_<kind>`. SITEMAP_PRODUCER_KINDS can limit which kinds emit (e.g. `SITEMAP_PRODUCER_KINDS=brand` for a brand-only first run).
_scheduledLanes_note: BUY-34838: sixth cron (lanes-producer, every 30min `37 */1 * * *`, `npm run producer:lanes`). The minute `37` is off the :00/:30 peak and offset from the other fleet-wide crons (Shopify `0 */6`, cc `17 */6`, WC `0 3 *`, Tranco `47 4 *`, sitemap `7 5 *`). Same caveat — Railway's `railway.json` only honors one `scheduled` key per deploy, so this entry MUST be added via the Railway dashboard on the buywhere-ingest service. Emits `scrape.shopify.lane.<role>` jobs (one per (role, domain) pair — 5 jobs per candidate, default 500 candidates/tick = 2500 jobs/tick max). The worker (5 lane handlers in src/worker.js) fetches each merchant with the role-specific maxPages/pageDelay/qualityMode config from src/laneRunner.js, runs the role-specific quality filter, and on pass calls /v1/ingest/products directly. Replaces the buy30620-page-lane-runner.mjs and buy30620-scout-validate-lane.mjs keepalives in the `5bc984ee-…` workspace; the drain loop at `7fb55262-…/scripts/ingest_buy30620_lanes.py` will see no new NDJSON files once the cutover lands. Feed source precedence: `LANE_FEED_R2_URL` (R2-stored JSONL) > `LANE_FEED_FILE_PATH` (local) > existing `lane_feed` table. Hex to populate the feed (their BUY-33668 writer can INSERT into lane_feed directly, or stage the JSONL in R2 under `lanes/google_shopping_merchants.jsonl`).
